serving.natural_language_portion: One individual serving of Sweet Bunny Hopsters is approximately 120 g, representing a single-portion layered mousse cake as sold at Kazo Premium.

nutrient_source.title: Chocolate mousse cake — estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite: Cake, chocolate with frosting (SR Legacy ID 18101) and Mousse, chocolate (SR Legacy ID 19270), weighted for a layered mousse-ganache cake composition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for 'Sweet Bunny Hopsters'. Nutrient values are estimated by weighting USDA SR Legacy data for chocolate cake with frosting (FDCID 18101) and chocolate mousse (FDCID 19270) in an approximate 60:40 ratio reflecting the described composition of moist chocolate sponge, vanilla mousse, and chocolate ganache. Vanilla mousse layer modelled on USDA SR Legacy 'Pudding, vanilla, dry mix' and cream data. Values represent a reasonable central estimate; actual product values may differ. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Chocolate cake with frosting GI ~38 (glucose reference); vanilla sponge cake GI ~46; weighted composite estimated at GI 65 (glucose scale) for a rich mousse-ganache cake with high sugar and fat content.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sweet Bunny Hopsters. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on analogous chocolate cake and mousse cake entries from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). The high sugar content (32 g/100 g) and refined flour base support a medium GI classification. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (65 × 45.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 35.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (210 mg/100 g) and high sugar load contributing to caloric excess and weight gain
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a serving (approximately 60 g) given the moderate sodium content and high caloric density, and avoid frequent consumption as part of a sodium-controlled dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (32 g/100 g), medium glycemic index (65), and high glycemic load per serving (35.1 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat Sweet Bunny Hopsters as an occasional indulgence only; the high sugar content and medium GI produce a high glycemic load per serving that will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, and consumption should be paired with careful carbohydrate budgeting for the day.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (11.2 g/100 g), trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), cholesterol (85 mg/100 g), and high caloric density (370 kcal/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this dessert due to its high saturated fat and trans fat content, which are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and increased cardiovascular risk; occasional small portions are preferable to regular intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sweet Bunny Hopsters is a premium individual-portion layered dessert cake sold at Kazo Premium in Singapore (Funan outlet). It consists of three distinct layers: a moist chocolate sponge base, a smooth and airy vanilla mousse centre, and a rich chocolate ganache topping. The cake is designed to deliver a contrast of textures — the dense, fudgy sponge against the light, creamy mousse — and a balance of flavour profiles, with the bittersweet ganache complementing the sweet vanilla cream. It is marketed as a treat for both children and adults. Nutritionally, it is a calorie-dense dessert at approximately 370 kcal per 100 g, with high sugar (32 g/100 g), high saturated fat (11.2 g/100 g), and a medium glycemic index of approximately 65. A single 120 g serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 35 g, making it a high-impact food for blood glucose management. It is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or weight. The dessert contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, and is not suitable for vegans. It is vegetarian-friendly. From a clinical nutrition standpoint, it should be classified as an occasional treat and portion-controlled strictly when consumed by individuals with metabolic or cardiovascular conditions.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for Sweet Bunny Hopsters. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous entries for chocolate cake with frosting and chocolate mousse, weighted by approximate layer composition. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for chocolate cake and mousse cake. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous entries (chocolate cake with frosting FDCID 18101; chocolate mousse FDCID 19270) in an approximate 60:40 ratio. Actual product values from Kazo Premium are not publicly available in any approved source database. 2. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for chocolate cake and mousse cake (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (5.8), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.9), trans_fat_g (0.2), potassium_mg (180.0), calcium_mg (65.0), iron_mg (1.4), cholesterol_mg (85.0), vitamin_c_mg (0.2), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (95.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.06), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.12), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (0.5), vitamin_b6_mg (0.04), vitamin_b12_mcg (0.18), folate_mcg_dfe (18.0), vitamin_d_mcg (0.4), vitamin_e_mg (0.7), phosphorus_mg (110.0), magnesium_mg (18.0), zinc_mg (0.5), selenium_mcg (5.0), and water_g (28.0) are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous data; no -1 sentinel values are used as reasonable estimates are available from the analogous source composite. 4. Halal status is listed as false because no halal certification information is publicly confirmed for Kazo Premium Funan; this should be verified with the restaurant directly. 5. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Mixed' (input) to 'Desserts' as this is a dessert cake product. 6. Local-language names are translational equivalents created for multilingual compliance; they are descriptive translations rather than established brand names in those languages.
